page: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64ls_v7 demonstrates strong compute capabilities, placing in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU performance. Its PassMark CPU score ranks in the top 10%, highlighting its raw processing power. The server also excels in data compression and decompression, with single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations reaching top-tier performance in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is robust, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth reaching 5133.04 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance achieves top-tier PassMark database scores and strong Redis SET performance at 113.0 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also highly efficient, ranking in the top 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D64ls_v7 offers high compute density relative to its memory footprint, making it a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive RAM arrays. However, the 2.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, and the lack of local storage requires external storage attachment. While LLM prompt processing speeds are strong, text generation performance falls into the average category. This server is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads such as high-throughput web serving, batch processing, data compression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes a 480 MB L3 cache with support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in PassMark CPU tests, data compression, and static web serving, alongside strong multi-core stress-ng and Redis SET operations. With its high compute density and moderate memory allocation, the D64ls_v7 is highly efficient for CPU-bound tasks. It is best suited for high-throughput web serving, data compression, batch processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier CPU performance, making it ideal for high-throughput web serving and batch processing.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, designed for high-throughput CPU-bound workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 128 GB RAM
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Top-tier performance in PassMark CPU, data compression, and web serving benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPUs, relying on external storage attachments
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, batch processing, and CPU-based inference
